title: argemone intermedia var. typica
intro: Argemone intermedia var. typica, also known as the Mexican prickly poppy, is a plant species belonging to the family Papaveraceae. The medicinal properties of Argemone intermedia var. typica include its seeds, which contain a toxic alkaloid called sanguinarine, as well as other compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant are said to include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions, although its toxicity requires caution and proper preparation. Herbal preparations of Argemone intermedia var. typica include infusions and decoctions of the plant's roots and seeds, which are often used in traditional medicine.
